name: The Drowned Coast
description: A maritime setting of flooded shores, wrecked galleons, salt-bleached villages, and things that follow ships home. Original content for The Lone Table.
credit: Signal9 Studio. Not affiliated with Tomkin Press.
locationPrompts: A tidal cave that floods in under an hour — something lives in the back of it
The wreck of the Ironsong, half-buried in black sand, listing hard to starboard
A salt-bleached fishing village built on stilts; three houses burned recently
A lighthouse no one maintains, but the light still turns every night
A kelp-choked sea-stack with a ruined stone tower at its peak
A shingle beach scattered with crates from a ship that sank six months ago
A smugglers' cove accessible only at low tide — two boats are hidden there now
A flooded chapel, its bell still audible on calm mornings
A sea-fort built on a sandbar that only appears at the lowest spring tides
A rope-bridge between two headlands, rotting, someone has crossed it recently
A whaling station long abandoned — the processing vats are full of something new
A graveyard of anchors, chains, and masts — the bones of a hundred ships

scenarioSeeds.title: The Ironsong
scenarioSeeds.vow: I will find out what happened to the Ironsong and bring the truth home
scenarioSeeds.hook: The Ironsong was a cargo vessel — grain, mostly, and a sealed crate no one was allowed near. It went down six weeks ago in clear weather. The only survivor refuses to speak. Something was on that ship that shouldn't have been, and the people who sent it want the truth buried with the wreck.
scenarioSeeds.firstScene: You stand on the beach below the cliff where the wreck is visible at low tide. A man watches you from fifty yards away. He leaves when he sees you notice him.

scenarioSeeds.title: The Light That Turns
scenarioSeeds.vow: I will discover what keeps the lighthouse burning and end it
scenarioSeeds.hook: The lighthouse keeper died three winters ago. The keeper's log was found on the beach, waterlogged, the last entry smeared. But the light still turns, every night, drawing ships onto the rocks — six this year alone.
scenarioSeeds.firstScene: You reach the lighthouse at dusk. The door is unlocked. The mechanism at the top is turning. There is no oil in the lamp.

scenarioSeeds.title: The Crate
scenarioSeeds.vow: I will find the sealed crate and decide what to do with what's inside
scenarioSeeds.hook: Before the Ironsong went down, a dockworker says he saw the crate move. Not shift — move. Three people have died trying to retrieve it from the wreck. The merchant who commissioned it has hired you now, offering more coin than you've seen in a year, and won't say what's inside.
scenarioSeeds.firstScene: The merchant meets you in a back room above a chandler's. She slides a key across the table. 'Don't open it until you've brought it to me. Don't open it under any circumstances.'

scenarioSeeds.title: The Stilt Village
scenarioSeeds.vow: I will find out who burned the houses and protect what remains of this place
scenarioSeeds.hook: Three houses burned three nights ago. No one saw who lit them. The village of Cassock Reach has eight families left — the others left last winter saying the tides were wrong. The remaining families are terrified and won't say of what.
scenarioSeeds.firstScene: The village elder is a woman of seventy who speaks in short sentences. She hands you a tide-chart with dates circled in charcoal. 'They come when the tide does,' she says. 'Next time is three days.'

scenarioSeeds.title: The Drowned Bell
scenarioSeeds.vow: I will silence the bell beneath the chapel and lay to rest what rings it
scenarioSeeds.hook: The flooded chapel has been submerged for thirty years, since the sea-wall broke. The bell should be rusted solid. But on calm mornings it rings, and the fishing boats that go out after it rings don't come back.
scenarioSeeds.firstScene: You hear it at dawn from the headland. One long note, then silence. Down in the harbour, two fishermen are readying a boat. Their faces are blank.

scenarioSeeds.title: The Spring Tide
scenarioSeeds.vow: I will be here when the sea-fort reappears and claim what it holds
scenarioSeeds.hook: Once every nineteen years, the lowest spring tide reveals the sea-fort on the Coldbar sandbar for six hours. The last time it surfaced, four people went out to it. One came back, said nothing, and died within the week. The next low water is in three days.
scenarioSeeds.firstScene: You've made camp on the headland above the bar. In the grey pre-dawn you can see something breaking the surface — angular, stone, definitely artificial. A boat is already heading out.

customOracles.name: Tide & Weather [Drowned Coast]
entries: Dead calm — the sea is black glass, nothing moves
Sea mist rolling in fast, visibility dropping to twenty yards
Spring tide — the water is higher than expected, paths are cut off
Storm building on the horizon, six hours away at most
Neap tide — the water is low, things usually hidden are briefly visible
Rain, heavy, the kind that fills boots and dulls sound
Fog bank sitting on the headland, showing no sign of lifting
Wind picking up from the north — cold, salt-laden, carrying the smell of deep water
A lull between squalls — window of clear weather, not long
Rip current running along the beach, dangerous to swim, stronger than it looks

customOracles.name: Coastal Encounter [Drowned Coast]
entries: A ship's boat washed up with supplies intact but no sign of the crew
A figure standing at the waterline, not moving, facing the sea
Wreckage from something recent — the wood is barely waterlogged
A fisherman who won't say where he's been or what he caught
Smugglers moving crates along the beach at speed — they see you
A child standing on the cliff above, watching, gone when you look again
A sealed chest half-buried in shingle, lock intact
The body of a sailor, no wounds, expression of terror
A flock of seabirds all facing the same direction in silence
Lights moving under the water in the shallows — no natural explanation
A local who claims to know what happened to the last group that came here
Fresh drag-marks in the sand, from the waterline into the treeline

customOracles.name: NPC — Drowned Coast [Drowned Coast]
entries: Harbourmaster, 60s, knows every ship that's passed through, owes a debt to someone dangerous
Fisherman's widow, pragmatic, will help you for practical reasons not sentimental ones
Merchant's factor, nervous, following orders they're uncomfortable with
Wreckers' lookout — young, not yet hardened, might be turned
Retired naval officer, drinks too much, knows the coast better than anyone alive
Rope-maker's apprentice, sees everything from the clifftop, doesn't know what it means
Customs inspector, ambitious, has his own investigation running
Kelp-cutter, works alone, has found things washed up they haven't told anyone about
Ship's cook, only survivor of something, won't discuss it but will talk around it
Village elder, last of the old families, keeps the old knowledge, not all of it useful

customOracles.name: Complication [Drowned Coast]
entries: The tide comes in faster than expected — a route back is cut off
The wreck shifts in the swell, something unsettled inside it
Someone else was here first — and left in a hurry
The mist closes in and the landmark you were navigating by disappears
A rope, board, or handhold fails at the worst possible moment
A boat you needed is gone — taken, sunk, or drifted
Someone in the village has sent word about you to the wrong people
The weather window closes — the storm arrives hours early
